question: Which Texas county is the county seat located in that meets all of the following: (1) between 700 and 4,600 people entering the state prison system in each fiscal year from 2019 to 2022, (2) population under 900,000 in the 2020 census, (3) not in the Houston metropolitan area, and (4) not in West Texas? Only report the county name in the format of `X County`. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: According to the Texas Department of Criminal Justice Receives FY 2019 data, 18 counties had between 700 and 4,600 criminal intakes, including Bexar (4,457), Hidalgo (1,698), Travis (1,649), El Paso (1,347), Montgomery (1,321), and 13 others.
1.subquestion: Which Texas counties had TDCJ criminal intakes between 700-4,600 in each fiscal year from FY 2019-2022? (Filter: group by County; count rows; keep 700 <= count <= 4600.)

2.fact: According to the Texas Department of Criminal Justice Receives FY 2020 data, 9 counties had between 700 and 4,600 criminal intakes: Harris (3,840), Tarrant (3,070), Dallas (2,519), Bexar (2,420), Hidalgo (1,088), Travis (981), Montgomery (843), McLennan (728), and El Paso (723).
2.subquestion: Which Texas counties had TDCJ criminal intakes between 700-4,600 in each fiscal year from FY 2019-2022? (Filter: group by County; count rows; keep 700 <= count <= 4600.)

3.fact: According to the Texas Department of Criminal Justice Receives FY 2021 data, 9 counties had between 700 and 4,600 criminal intakes: Dallas (3,520), Tarrant (2,869), Bexar (2,822), Hidalgo (946), Collin (851), Smith (823), Montgomery (821), El Paso (799), and Travis (705).
3.subquestion: Which Texas counties had TDCJ criminal intakes between 700-4,600 in each fiscal year from FY 2019-2022? (Filter: group by County; count rows; keep 700 <= count <= 4600.)

4.fact: According to the Texas Department of Criminal Justice Receives FY 2022 data, 11 counties had between 700 and 4,600 criminal intakes: Tarrant (3,699), Dallas (3,639), Bexar (3,240), Hidalgo (1,181), Montgomery (1,145), Smith (952), Jefferson (806), Collin (769), McLennan (732), El Paso (726), and Johnson (716).
4.subquestion: Which Texas counties had TDCJ criminal intakes between 700-4,600 in each fiscal year from FY 2019-2022? (Filter: group by County; count rows; keep 700 <= count <= 4600.)

5.fact: According to the 2020 Census Redistricting Data for Texas counties, among the hop 1 intersection {Bexar, El Paso, Hidalgo, Montgomery}: El Paso had population 865,657, Hidalgo had 870,781, and Montgomery had 620,443 (all under 900,000), while Bexar had 2,009,324 (over 900,000).
5.subquestion: Among <hop 1 answer: Bexar, El Paso, Hidalgo, Montgomery>, which counties have 2020 census population under 900,000? (Filter: SUMLEV=50; BASENAME in {Bexar, El Paso, Hidalgo, Montgomery}; POP100 < 900000.)

6.fact: According to the Wikipedia article for Montgomery County, Texas, "Montgomery County is part of the Houston–The Woodlands–Sugar Land metropolitan statistical area."
6.subquestion: Among <hop 2 answer: El Paso, Hidalgo, Montgomery>, which counties are NOT in the Houston metropolitan area?

7.fact: According to the Wikipedia article for West Texas, the list of West Texas counties includes El Paso.
7.subquestion: Among <hop 2 answer: El Paso, Hidalgo>, which county is in West Texas?
7.answer: El Paso County is in West Texas

8.fact: According to the Wikipedia article for Hidalgo County, Texas, 'It is located in the Rio Grande Valley of South Texas.' The county seat is Edinburg.
8.subquestion: Among <hop 2 answer: El Paso, Hidalgo>, which county is located in South Texas (not West Texas)?
8.answer: Hidalgo County is in South Texas, county seat is Edinburg

reasoning_chain: Node 1: FY 2019 intakes 700-4600 -> 18 counties including {Bexar, El Paso, Hidalgo, Montgomery, Travis, ...}
Node 2: FY 2020 intakes 700-4600 -> 9 counties: {Harris, Tarrant, Dallas, Bexar, Hidalgo, Travis, Montgomery, McLennan, El Paso}
Node 3: FY 2021 intakes 700-4600 -> 9 counties: {Dallas, Tarrant, Bexar, Hidalgo, Collin, Smith, Montgomery, El Paso, Travis}
Node 4: FY 2022 intakes 700-4600 -> 11 counties: {Tarrant, Dallas, Bexar, Hidalgo, Montgomery, Smith, Jefferson, Collin, McLennan, El Paso, Johnson}
Node 5 (2020 Census): Population under 900k -> eliminates Bexar -> {El Paso, Hidalgo, Montgomery}
Node 6 (Montgomery Wikipedia): In Houston metro -> eliminates Montgomery -> {El Paso, Hidalgo}
HOP 3 (d=2, Wikipedia region + county pages - nodes 7-8):
Node 7 (West Texas Wikipedia): El Paso listed among West Texas counties
Node 8 (Hidalgo Wikipedia): Located in South Texas, county seat is Edinburg
Result: Hidalgo County, county seat = Edinburg
